title: Mechanical end face seal having an induction heated seal face
abstract: An induction heated mechanical seal system. The induction heated mechanical seal system including a mechanical seal and inductive coil, the mechanical seal installed between a stationary housing in a rotatable shaft, the mechanical seal including a non-rotating sealing element operably coupled to the stationary housing, and a rotating sealing element operably coupled to the rotatable shaft, wherein the non-rotating sealing element and the rotating sealing element respectively define opposed radial seal surfaces of a sealing interface adapted for acceptance a lubricating film of the sealing fluid therein, the inductive coil operably coupled to the stationary housing and configured to produce a magnetic field to induce eddy currents in at least a portion of the non-rotating sealing element, thereby increasing the temperature of the non-rotating sealing element and adjacent sealing interface, so as to decrease a viscosity of the sealing fluid.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An induction heated mechanical seal system configured to decrease the viscosity of sealing fluid therein, the mechanical seal system comprising: a mechanical seal (10) installed between a stationary housing (11) and a rotatable shaft (13), the mechanical seal including a non-rotating sealing element (19) operably coupled to the stationary housing (11), and a rotating sealing element (14) operably coupled to the rotatable shaft (13), wherein the non-rotating sealing element (19) and rotating sealing element (14) respectively define opposed radial seal surfaces (24, 31) of a sealing interface (20) adapted for acceptance a lubricating film of the sealing fluid therein; an inductive coil (43) operably coupled to the stationary housing (11), the inductive coil (43) configured to produce a magnetic field to induce eddy currents in at least a portion of the non-rotating sealing element (19), thereby increasing the temperature of the non- rotating sealing element (19) and adjacent sealing interface (20), so as to decrease the viscosity of the sealing fluid. 2. The mechanical seal system of claim 1, wherein the non-rotating sealing element (19) is fabricated of a magnetically susceptible metal material capable of being inductively heated. 3. The mechanical seal system of claim 2, wherein the non-rotating sealing element (19) is fabricated of iron, stainless steel, titanium, or an alloy thereof. 4. The mechanical seal system of claim 1, wherein the portion of the stationary housing (11) in which the inductive coil (43) is operably coupled is fabricated of a non-magnetically susceptible metal material to minimize inductive heating of the stationary housing (11). 5. The mechanical seal system of claim 4, wherein the portion of the stationary housing (11) in which the inductive coil (43) is operably coupled is fabricated of aluminum, copper, austentic stainless steel, or an alloy thereof. 6. The mechanical seal system of claim 1, wherein the inductive coil (43) at least partially surrounds a portion of the non-rotating sealing element (19). 7. The mechanical seal system of claim 1, wherein the inductive coil (43) at least partially surrounds the rotatable shaft (13) proximal to an end of the non-rotating sealing element (19) opposite to the radial seal surface (24). 8. The mechanical seal system of claim 1, further comprising a power source (44) configured to supply an alternating high-frequency current to the induction coil (43). 9. The mechanical seal system of claim 8, further comprising a sensing device (49) configured to sense a temperature of the non-rotating sealing element (19). 10. The mechanical seal system of claim 9, further comprising a power source controller (46) configured to adjust an output of the power source (44) based on the sensed temperature by the sensing device (49), so as to maintain the non-rotating sealing element (19) at a desired temperature. 11. The mechanical seal system of claim 1, wherein the radial seal surface (24) of the non-rotating sealing element (19) includes a high-hardness abrasion resistant coating metallurgically bonded to the non-rotating sealing element (19). 12. The mechanical seal system of claim 1, wherein the rotating sealing element (14) is fabricated of carbon graphite. 13. The mechanical seal system of claim 1, wherein the radial seal surface (31) of the rotating sealing element (14) is fabricated of a material having a hardness harder than the radial seal surface (24) of the non-rotating sealing element (19). 14. The mechanical seal system of claim 1, wherein the radial seal surface (31) of the rotating sealing element (14) is fabricated of silicon carbide.
